prerequisites: A Google Cloud project with the Google Sheets API enabled in the Google Cloud console.
The spreadsheet_id of the target Google Sheets file, taken from the sheet URL between /d/ and the next slash: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SPREADSHEET_ID/edit.
Credentials for one of the supported auth methods (an OAuth client plus refresh token, or a service account key) with access to the spreadsheet.
An OAuth scope that permits reading the sheet, such as https://www.googleapis.com/auth/spreadsheets.readonly (read-only) or the broader spreadsheets / drive scopes.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (client ID, client secret, refresh token)
authMethods.summary: Provide credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret from an OAuth client created under APIs & Services > Credentials in the Google Cloud console, plus a credentials.refresh_token obtained by completing the OAuth consent flow. Google exchanges the refresh token for short-lived access tokens to read the spreadsheet.

authMethods.name: Service account
authMethods.summary: Provide credentials.service_account_info as the JSON key of a Google Cloud service account. Create the service account under IAM & Admin > Service Accounts, generate a JSON key, and share the target spreadsheet with the service account's email so it can read the data.

setupSteps.title: Enable the Google Sheets API
setupSteps.body: In the Google Cloud console, select or create a project and enable the Google Sheets API for it. This is required before any credentials can call the API.

setupSteps.title: Create credentials for your chosen auth method
setupSteps.body: For OAuth, open APIs & Services > Credentials and create an OAuth client to get a client ID and client secret, then run the consent flow to obtain a refresh token. For a service account, open IAM & Admin > Service Accounts, create a service account, and generate a JSON key. If you use a service account, share the spreadsheet with the service account email address so it can access the file.

setupSteps.title: Find the spreadsheet ID
setupSteps.body: Open the target sheet in your browser and copy the spreadsheet_id from the URL. It is the segment between /d/ and the next slash in https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/SPREADSHEET_ID/edit. The ID stays stable even if you rename the file.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Set spreadsheet_id and the credentials object (OAuth client_id/client_secret/refresh_token or service_account_info). Optionally tune the header-name handling fields (names_conversion, allow_leading_numbers, combine_letter_number_pairs, combine_number_word_pairs, remove_leading_trailing_underscores, remove_special_characters, read_empty_header_columns), batch_size, num_workers, and stream_name_overrides as needed.

sources.title: Google Sheets API concepts (spreadsheet ID in URL)
Using OAuth 2.0 to Access Google APIs
